decomposition_notes: Single-goal shape — all work lives in app.py (the only file the constraint permits modifying). The two deliverables (status bar and depot summary table) are code-coupled: both read from the same depot_results pipeline in app.py and both live in the Overview tab block. Five tasks ordered by dependency: (1) capture scan timestamp in session state, (2) render status bar, (3) add _build_depot_summary() helper, (4) render the Depot-Ubersicht table, (5) run full test suite to verify 193 tests green. Tasks 1 and 2 are independent of 0, allowing some parallelism. Task 3 depends on both 1 and 2. Task 4 depends on all prior tasks.

goals.title: Add Overview status bar and Depot-Ubersicht summary table to app.py
goals.why: Users must navigate to each depot tab individually to assess depot health with no single view for side-by-side comparison, and there is no persistent freshness indicator to confirm data currency. Both gaps are addressed by the status bar and per-depot summary table on the Overview tab.
goals.what: Modify app.py to: (1) capture a scan-completion timestamp in session state after the screening loop, (2) render a one-line status bar at the top of the Overview tab showing scan time, last-scan datetime, auto-scan countdown (static), and a Re-scan button, (3) add _build_depot_summary() helper that computes per-depot summary rows from depot_results, (4) render the Depot-Ubersicht table with colour-coded signal cells and a GESAMT totals row. No changes to screener.py, indicators.py, or classifier.py.
goals.where_context: app.py only — Section 3 screening loop (timestamp capture), Overview tab block Section 4 (status bar, summary table). All five tasks touch only app.py.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
After scan, session state contains scan_completed_at with a datetime value
Overview tab shows a one-line status bar: check-mark icon + scan time + last-scan datetime + auto-scan interval + Re-scan button
Re-scan button in status bar clears screen_ and depot_ session state keys and calls st.rerun()
Depot-Ubersicht table appears with columns: Depot | Stocks | Score O | Buy | Bullish | Neutral | Bearish | Sell-Panic | Errors
One row per depot plus GESAMT totals row at the bottom
Signal tier count cells with value > 0 are colour-coded using existing _SIGNAL_COLOURS palette
Score O is mean RS Rating of non-None values, displayed to 1 decimal place
Errors = count of failed_isins for the depot
Table renders correctly in both depot_config.json mode and single-depot auto-discovery fallback
All 193 existing tests remain green
No new pip dependencies
pitfalls: depot_results contains (results, excluded) tuples; index alignment with the depots list is load-bearing — verify alignment before zipping
Score O must exclude rows where RS Rating is None; a mean over all rows will produce NaN silently if any row has None
The _gk() widget key manager must be used for any new button or widget added in the Overview tab to prevent DuplicateElementKey errors on re-run
The auto-scan countdown is a static display of the configured interval — do not attempt a live countdown that requires JS injection or a new dependency
st.dataframe does not natively apply per-cell background colours; use df.style with a map/applymap call mirroring the existing _styled() pattern — do not use st.table
Pandas Styler format() for Score O must handle None values — use na_rep='-' to avoid TypeError

tasks.title: Capture scan_completed_at timestamp in session state after screening loop
tasks.description: Immediately after the _screen_prog.empty() call that concludes the Section 3 screening loop in app.py, write the current datetime into st.session_state under the key 'scan_completed_at'. Guard the write with 'scan_completed_at' not in st.session_state so cached re-renders do not overwrite the real scan time. Add 'import datetime' at the top of app.py if not already present.
tasks.why: The status bar must show a scan-completion timestamp. Writing it once at the point of completion ensures the timestamp reflects the actual scan time and survives tab switches without being reset.
tasks.what: In app.py: add 'import datetime' at top if missing; after _screen_prog.empty(), add: if 'scan_completed_at' not in st.session_state: st.session_state['scan_completed_at'] = datetime.datetime.now()
tasks.where_context: app.py — import block (top of file); Section 3 screening loop ending near _screen_prog.empty()
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
datetime is imported at the top of app.py
st.session_state['scan_completed_at'] is set to a datetime.datetime object after scan completes
The assignment is guarded: only executes when 'scan_completed_at' is not already in session state
screener.py, indicators.py, classifier.py are unmodified
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing session state guard pattern: check 'key' not in st.session_state before writing
Use datetime.datetime.now() — no timezone library, no new dependency
pitfalls: Do not place the write inside the per-depot screening loop — it must execute once after all depots are screened
Do not clear scan_completed_at in the Re-scan session state clear block — it will be overwritten on the next completed scan

key_files.note: Add datetime import at top; add scan_completed_at write after _screen_prog.empty()

verification_steps.step_text: Run 'streamlit run app.py', complete a scan, confirm scan_completed_at appears in Streamlit session state inspector with a datetime value
verification_steps.expected_result: scan_completed_at key present with datetime.datetime value

verification_steps.step_text: Click Re-scan and confirm the timestamp updates to a new value after the next scan completes
verification_steps.expected_result: scan_completed_at updated to new scan time

manual_tests: cached page load does not overwrite timestamp
edge_cases: Cached page load (no new scan) — timestamp must NOT be overwritten

tasks.title: Render status bar at top of Overview tab
tasks.description: At the top of the Overview tab block (with tabs[0]:) in app.py, before the existing metrics columns, render a single-line status bar. Show: (1) check-mark + 'Scan completed HH:MM:SS' from st.session_state.get('scan_completed_at'), (2) 'Last scan: DD.MM.YYYY HH:MM:SS', (3) 'Next auto-scan in X min' where X is from session state or defaults to 60, (4) a Re-scan button using _gk('rescan_overview') that clears screen_ and depot_ keys and calls st.rerun(). Show a placeholder when scan_completed_at is absent.
tasks.why: Users need a persistent freshness indicator on the Overview tab to assess data currency without navigating to the sidebar.
tasks.what: Insert st.columns layout at start of 'with tabs[0]:' block. Read scan_completed_at from session state. Format as check-mark + time + last-scan + auto-scan interval. Add Re-scan button. If scan_completed_at is not yet set, show a waiting placeholder.
tasks.where_context: app.py — inside 'with tabs[0]:' block, before the existing ov_c1/ov_c2/ov_c3/ov_c4 metrics row
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Status bar is the first element inside tabs[0]
Shows check-mark, scan time (HH:MM:SS), last-scan full datetime (DD.MM.YYYY HH:MM:SS), and static auto-scan interval
Re-scan button clears session state keys starting with 'screen_' and 'depot_' and calls st.rerun()
_gk() is used for the Re-scan button key
If scan_completed_at is absent, a placeholder is shown without KeyError
Status bar does NOT appear on per-depot tabs
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Reuse the existing Re-scan button logic from the sidebar — same session state key clearing
Use st.columns for layout; no raw HTML or JS injection
Read refresh_mins from session state with a default of 60 if not present
pitfalls: The Re-scan button here and the sidebar Re-scan button must clear the same set of session state keys
refresh_mins is set inside the sidebar block; read from session state or use a module-level default to avoid NameError in the tabs block
Do not use st.components.v1.html — no JS needed for static display

key_files.note: Overview tab block — insert status bar before existing metrics columns

verification_steps.step_text: Run app, complete a scan, navigate to Overview tab — status bar with timestamp and Re-scan button is visible as first element
verification_steps.expected_result: Status bar visible with formatted timestamp and Re-scan button

verification_steps.step_text: Click Re-scan button in status bar — app re-runs and re-scans
verification_steps.expected_result: App re-runs; screening progress bar appears

verification_steps.step_text: Navigate to a per-depot tab — confirm status bar does NOT appear there
verification_steps.expected_result: No status bar on per-depot tabs

edge_cases: scan_completed_at absent from session state — must not raise KeyError

tasks.title: Add _build_depot_summary() helper function to app.py
tasks.description: Add a module-level helper function _build_depot_summary(depots, depot_results) to app.py after the _styled() helper. Accepts the depots list (dicts with 'name', 'tickers', 'failed_isins') and the depot_results list (list of (results, excluded) tuples, index-aligned with depots). Returns a list of dicts with keys: Depot, Stocks, 'Score O', Buy, Bullish, Neutral, Bearish, 'Sell-Panic', Errors. Appends a GESAMT totals row. Uses SIGNAL_ORDER from classifier.py to enumerate tiers.
tasks.why: Isolating aggregation logic in a testable function keeps the Overview tab render block readable and the aggregation independently verifiable without Streamlit dependencies.
tasks.what: Define _build_depot_summary(depots, depot_results) in app.py. For each (depot, (results, excluded)) pair: Stocks = len(results); Score O = mean of non-None RS Ratings (None if list empty); each signal tier count = sum(1 for r in results if r.get('Signal') == tier); Errors = len(depot['failed_isins']). Append GESAMT row summing int columns and averaging Score O values.
tasks.where_context: app.py — define after _styled() helper function, before Section 4 Overview tab block
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
_build_depot_summary is defined in app.py before the Section 4 block
Returns a list of dicts; last entry has Depot == 'GESAMT'
Stocks = len(results) for each depot
Score O is mean of non-None RS Rating values to 1 decimal, or None if no RS Ratings
Each signal tier column counts correctly
Errors = len(depot['failed_isins'])
GESAMT row sums all int columns; Score O is mean of per-depot Score O values (excluding None)
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use SIGNAL_ORDER from classifier (already imported in app.py) for stable tier enumeration
Use list comprehensions rather than pandas for the aggregation to keep the function dependency-light
pitfalls: depots and depot_results are index-aligned; verify alignment before iterating — do not use zip if it could be disrupted by the upload flow
Score O must be None (not NaN or 0) when no stock has a valid RS Rating
GESAMT Score O should average per-depot means, not re-average all individual stocks

key_files.note: SIGNAL_ORDER consumed by the helper for stable tier enumeration

verification_steps.step_text: In a Python REPL, import or paste _build_depot_summary, construct synthetic depots and depot_results with known values, verify returned list has one row per depot plus GESAMT with correct totals
verification_steps.expected_result: Returned list length = len(depots) + 1; GESAMT row sums match

verification_steps.step_text: Pass a depot with all RS Rating = None in results, verify Score O is None in the returned row
verification_steps.expected_result: Score O is None for that depot row

unit_tests: Synthetic depots and depot_results with known counts — assert returned rows match expected values
Assert Score O = None when all RS Ratings are None
manual_tests: REPL smoke test with real depot_results after a scan

tasks.title: Render Depot-Ubersicht table with colour-coded signal cells in Overview tab
tasks.description: In the Overview tab block, after the status bar and before the existing Buy Signals subheader, call _build_depot_summary(depots, depot_results), convert to DataFrame, apply per-cell background colours to signal tier columns (non-zero cells get the tier's colour from _SIGNAL_COLOURS; zero cells are unstyled), and render with st.dataframe(styler, use_container_width=True). Add st.subheader('Depot-Ubersicht') above the table. Format Score O to 1 decimal with na_rep='-'.
tasks.why: This is the primary deliverable — the at-a-glance depot health table from the Outcome section. Placing it before the Buy Signals section matches the intended reading order.
tasks.what: After status bar code: call _build_depot_summary, build DataFrame, define colour function that applies _SIGNAL_COLOURS[tier] to cells > 0 and empty string to zero/None cells, apply styles via df.style.map or df.style.apply, format Score O column, render. Visually distinguish GESAMT row (e.g. bold Depot cell).
tasks.where_context: app.py — inside 'with tabs[0]:' block, after status bar code, before existing ov_c1/ov_c2/ov_c3/ov_c4 metrics row or directly before 'Buy Signals' subheader
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
st.subheader('Depot-Ubersicht') appears above the table
Columns in order: Depot, Stocks, Score O, Buy, Bullish, Neutral, Bearish, Sell-Panic, Errors
One row per depot plus GESAMT row at bottom
Non-zero Buy cells have green background matching _SIGNAL_COLOURS['Buy']
Non-zero Bullish cells have light green background
Non-zero Neutral cells have yellow background
Non-zero Bearish cells have light red background
Non-zero Sell-Panic cells have red background
Zero and None cells have no colour
Score O displayed to 1 decimal (None shown as '-')
Table renders in single-depot auto-discovery fallback without errors
No DuplicateElementKey error on re-run
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror existing _styled() function pattern using df.style map/applymap
Use use_container_width=True consistent with all other table renders in app.py
Do not use st.table
pitfalls: Pandas Styler format() for Score O must handle None — use format('{:.1f}', na_rep='-')
The GESAMT row mixes str (Depot) and int/float columns — ensure int columns are not coerced to float by a None Score O in the GESAMT row
Colour function must check value > 0, not just the column name — zero count in Buy column must not be green

key_files.note: Overview tab block — add Depot-Ubersicht table render after status bar

verification_steps.step_text: Run app, complete scan with 2+ depots, navigate to Overview tab — Depot-Ubersicht table visible with one row per depot plus GESAMT
verification_steps.expected_result: Table visible with correct row count and GESAMT row

verification_steps.step_text: Confirm Buy column cells with value > 0 have green background; cells with 0 have no colour
verification_steps.expected_result: Non-zero Buy cells green; zero Buy cells unstyled

verification_steps.step_text: Remove depot_config.json, re-run app, confirm table renders with one depot row plus GESAMT
Confirm Score O shows one decimal place (e.g. 82.3 not 82.333)

manual_tests: Signal tier colour coding correct for non-zero cells
Single-depot fallback mode renders without error

tasks.title: Verify all 193 existing tests remain green after Overview tab changes
tasks.description: Run the full test suite to confirm that none of the changes in tasks 0-3 have broken existing tests. The constraint states screener.py, indicators.py, and classifier.py must not be modified; this task confirms that constraint was honoured and that no app.py changes introduced import errors or side effects visible to tests.
tasks.why: The requirements doc explicitly states all 193 existing tests must remain green. This is a hard constraint.
tasks.what: Run pytest from the project root targeting tests/. Confirm all tests pass. If any test fails, fix the root cause in app.py; do not modify test files to mask failures.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
pytest tests/ exits with 0 failures
Test count is 193 or more
No test in test_classifier.py, test_screener.py, or test_indicators.py fails
No import errors from any test module caused by app.py changes
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Run pytest without modification flags — do not skip or deselect tests
Fix root causes in app.py; do not modify test files
pitfalls: app.py imports classifier at module level — if the import was refactored, confirm classifier.py was not modified
_build_depot_summary added to app.py must not have introduced any module-level side effects that interfere with test imports

key_files.note: Must remain green — classifier.py not modified
Must remain green — screener.py not modified
Must remain green — indicators.py not modified

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass; final line shows 193 passed (or higher); 0 failures, 0 errors
All tests in the three protected-module test files pass with 0 failures

unit_tests: Run full existing test suite without modification
edge_cases: Import errors from app.py changes bleeding into test collection
